name: Credit Counseling Of Arkansas - Bentonville
description_short: Non-profit credit counseling agency offering free budget, credit, and housing counseling since 1995 with HUD certification and NFCC affiliation.
description_long:
Credit Counseling of Arkansas (CCOA), Inc. is a non-profit organization established in 1995 that has served thousands of families nationwide. The organization operates multiple office locations including Bentonville, Fayetteville, and Fort Smith, making services accessible across Arkansas. CCOA is HUD-certified for housing counseling and affiliated with the National Foundation for Credit Counseling (NFCC), which reflects its adherence to professional standards and ethics in financial counseling.

CCOA offers comprehensive financial counseling services at no cost, including budget counseling, credit report reviews, debt management programs, and housing-related counseling (first-time homebuyer, mortgage delinquency, and reverse mortgage counseling). They provide pre-filing bankruptcy counseling and free financial education seminars. Their Debt Management Program specifically aims to reduce interest rates, stop collection fees, and halt collection calls. Clients can access services through multiple channels: in-person appointments, phone consultations, or email for budget and debt management counseling.

What distinguishes CCOA is their comprehensive scope of free services without obligation, their HUD and NFCC certifications, and their measurable client outcomes. The organization reports that 93% of clients gain better control of finances, 94% apply information from financial classes to their practices, and 97% feel closer to becoming debt-free. They also highlight client success stories, including one CCOA client receiving the NFCC Brighter Financial Future national award, demonstrating real-world impact.

As a non-profit with legitimate credentials, CCOA appears genuinely designed to help consumers manage debt and improve financial situations without profit incentive. The primary limitation is that they primarily offer counseling and debt management programs rather than direct debt elimination or settlement, so they work best for those seeking guidance and structured repayment rather than aggressive debt negotiation. Their services require commitment to financial planning rather than quick fixes.
pros: Completely free financial consultations and counseling with no obligation
HUD-certified housing counseling agency offering specialized services (first-time homebuyer, mortgage delinquency, reverse mortgage counseling)
NFCC-affiliated with certified financial counselors meeting professional standards
Multiple service delivery options including in-person, phone, and email counseling
Debt Management Program specifically designed to reduce interest rates and stop collection calls
Documented client outcomes: 97% report feeling closer to debt-free status
Operating since 1995 with established track record helping thousands of families
cons: Debt Management Program is debt consolidation/management, not settlement — requires longer repayment timelines rather than reducing principal owed
Limited to counseling and guidance services; cannot directly negotiate or eliminate debt like settlement companies
Identity verification required for credit report reviews, which adds an extra step to initial consultations
Email counseling only available for budget and debt management, not all service types
No indication of availability for emergency same-day assistance or rapid funding options
best_for: Consumers seeking free credit counseling and budget planning without financial pressure
First-time homebuyers needing HUD-certified pre-purchase housing counseling
Individuals in mortgage delinquency looking for specialized housing counseling assistance
People with multiple debts seeking structured management and interest rate reduction through formal programs
diagnosis: CCOA is best for consumers who need structured financial guidance, want to reduce debt through managed repayment plans, or require HUD-certified housing counseling — particularly those avoiding predatory lenders or high-cost debt solutions. The key caveat is that they offer counseling and debt management programs, not debt settlement or elimination, so they work best for people ready to commit to long-term financial recovery rather than seeking quick debt reduction.
services: Debt Management Program with interest rate reduction
Credit improvement advice from certified counselors
Money skills building through free educational resources
Credit card, medical, and housing debt assistance
Telephone, in-person, and email counseling options
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
